title: chamaesyce bracei
intro: Chamaesyce bracei, also known as the Hawaiian spurge or sandmat, is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae). It is a succulent plant native to Hawaii, with medicinal properties attributed to its latex and sap, which contain alkaloids and other compounds with potential health benefits. Chamaesyce bracei has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and skin conditions, with its extracts showing anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Chamaesyce bracei include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
